name: Amoxicillin 500mg
indications: Bacterial infections of the respiratory tract, urinary tract, skin and soft tissue
dosage: 500 mg orally three times daily for 7–10 days

name: Azithromycin 250mg
indications: Community-acquired pneumonia, pharyngitis, tonsillitis, skin infections
dosage: 500 mg on Day 1, then 250 mg once daily on Days 2–5

name: Ciprofloxacin 500mg
indications: Urinary tract infections, lower respiratory tract infections, enteric fever
dosage: 500 mg orally twice daily for 7–14 days

name: Metronidazole 200mg
indications: Anaerobic bacterial infections, amoebiasis, giardiasis, Helicobacter pylori eradication
dosage: 200–400 mg orally three times daily for 7 days

name: Paracetamol 500mg
indications: Mild to moderate pain relief; fever reduction
dosage: 500–1000 mg every 4–6 hours; max 4 g per day

name: Ibuprofen 400mg
indications: Pain, inflammation, fever; arthritis, dysmenorrhoea
dosage: 400 mg orally every 6–8 hours with food; max 1200 mg/day (OTC)

name: Tramadol 50mg
indications: Moderate to moderately severe acute and chronic pain
dosage: 50–100 mg orally every 4–6 hours; max 400 mg/day

name: Oseltamivir 75mg
indications: Treatment and prophylaxis of influenza A and B
dosage: 75 mg twice daily for 5 days (treatment); 75 mg once daily for 10 days (prophylaxis)

name: Acyclovir 200mg
indications: Herpes simplex infections, herpes zoster (shingles), varicella (chickenpox)
dosage: 200 mg five times daily for 5 days; adjust for renal impairment

name: Amlodipine 5mg
indications: Hypertension, stable and vasospastic angina pectoris
dosage: 5 mg once daily; may be increased to 10 mg once daily

name: Metformin 500mg
indications: Type 2 diabetes mellitus; polycystic ovary syndrome (off-label)
dosage: 500 mg twice or three times daily with meals; max 2550 mg/day

name: Atorvastatin 20mg
indications: Hypercholesterolaemia, mixed dyslipidaemia, cardiovascular risk reduction
dosage: 10–80 mg once daily at any time of day

name: Omeprazole 20mg
indications: Peptic ulcer disease, GERD, H. pylori eradication (with antibiotics), Zollinger-Ellison syndrome
dosage: 20–40 mg once daily before a meal; 4–8 week course

name: Loratadine 10mg
indications: Allergic rhinitis, urticaria, hay fever
dosage: 10 mg once daily

name: Salbutamol 100mcg Inhaler
indications: Acute bronchospasm, asthma, COPD — short-acting bronchodilator (reliever)
dosage: 100–200 mcg (1–2 puffs) as needed; max 8 puffs/day

name: Prednisolone 5mg
indications: Inflammatory and allergic conditions, asthma, autoimmune diseases, transplant rejection prevention
dosage: Initial: 5–60 mg/day; taper dose on long-term use

name: Vitamin C 1000mg
indications: Prevention and treatment of Vitamin C deficiency; antioxidant supplementation
dosage: 1000 mg once daily

name: Vitamin D3 5000IU
indications: Vitamin D deficiency, osteoporosis prophylaxis, rickets
dosage: 5000 IU once daily; higher doses under medical supervision

name: Omega-3 1000mg
indications: Hypertriglyceridaemia, cardiovascular protection, anti-inflammatory supplementation
dosage: 1000 mg once to three times daily with meals

name: Sildenafil 50mg
indications: Erectile dysfunction; pulmonary arterial hypertension (higher doses)
dosage: 50 mg approximately 1 hour before sexual activity; range 25–100 mg
